subjects.name: Numerical Techniques and Statistical Methods

units.title: Iterative Methods
topics: Solutions of algebraic and transcendental equations: bisection, secant, false position and iteration methods
Newton-Raphson method (one variable and simultaneous equations)
Interpolation: Newton's forward and backward formulae
Interpolation with unequal intervals; Lagrange's interpolation formula

units.title: Numerical Integration and Solution of ODEs
topics: Trapezoidal rule; Simpson's 1/3 and 3/8 rules
Solution of initial value problems by Taylor's series and Picard's method
Euler's method
Runge-Kutta method (second and fourth order); Milne's predictor-corrector method

units.title: Probability and Distributions
topics: Baye's theorem; discrete and continuous random variables
Distribution functions: probability mass function, probability density function and cumulative distribution functions
Mathematical expectation and variance
Binomial, Poisson, Uniform and Normal distributions

units.title: Sampling Theory
topics: Population and samples; sampling distribution of means and variance
Point and interval estimations; maximum error of estimate
Central limit theorem (without proof)
Estimation using t, chi-square and F distributions

units.title: Tests of Hypothesis
topics: Hypothesis; null and alternative hypothesis; Type I and Type II errors
Level of significance; one-tail and two-tail tests
Test of significance for large and small samples: single and difference of means, single and two proportions
Student's t-test, F-test and chi-square test

seo.title: Numerical Techniques & Statistical Methods - CE 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 CE 2-1 Numerical Techniques & Statistical Methods: iterative methods, integration & ODEs, distributions, sampling and hypothesis tests.

subjects.name: Surveying

units.title: Introduction and Basic Concepts
topics: Classification and principles of surveying; surveying accessories; introduction to compass, levelling and plane table surveying
Linear distances: approximate and direct methods; chains, tapes, ranging, tape corrections
Prismatic compass: bearings, included angles
Local attraction, magnetic declination and dip; WCB and QB systems of locating bearings

units.title: Levelling, Contouring, Areas and Volumes
topics: Levelling: types of levels, methods and determination of levels; effect of curvature and refraction
Contouring: characteristics and uses of contours, methods of contour surveying
Areas: determination of areas with irregular and regular boundaries
Volumes: earthwork in cutting and embankments for level section, capacity of reservoirs

units.title: Theodolite Surveying and Traversing
topics: Types of theodolites and temporary adjustments
Measurement of horizontal angle by repetition and reiteration methods; vertical angle measurement
Trigonometrical levelling when base is accessible and inaccessible
Traversing: methods, traverse computations and adjustments, omitted measurements

units.title: Curves and Modern Surveying Methods
topics: Types of curves and their necessity; elements of simple, compound and reverse curves
Introduction to tacheometric surveying
EDM instruments; total station - advantages and applications

units.title: Photogrammetry Surveying
topics: Basic concepts; perspective geometry of an aerial photograph; relief and tilt displacements

topics: Elasticity and plasticity; types of stresses and strains; Hooke's law
Factor of safety; Poisson's ratio
Relationship between elastic constants
Bars of varying section; stresses in composite bars

units.title: Shear Force and Bending Moment
topics: Definition and types of beams; point of contraflexure
Relation between shear force, bending moment and rate of loading
SF and BM diagrams for cantilever, simply supported and overhanging beams
Beams under point loads, UDL, uniformly varying loads, partial UDL, couple and combinations

units.title: Flexural and Shear Stresses & Torsion
topics: Theory of simple bending: assumptions, derivation of bending equation, neutral axis
Section modulus of rectangular, circular (solid and hollow), I, T, angle and channel sections; design of beams

Torsion of circular shafts

units.title: Deflection of Beams
topics: Double integration and Macaulay's methods

units.title: Columns, Thin and Thick Cylinders
topics: Classification of columns; Euler's crippling load theory for various end conditions; equivalent length and slenderness ratio
Limitations of Euler's theory; Rankine-Gordon formula; eccentric loading, secant and Perry's formula
Thin cylindrical shells: longitudinal and circumferential stresses; hoop, longitudinal and volumetric strains
Lame's theory for thick cylinders; distribution of hoop and radial stresses; compound cylinders

Vapor pressure, surface tension, capillarity, bulk modulus of elasticity, compressibility

Piezometer, U-tube, single column and differential manometers; pressure gauges

units.title: Fluid Statics
topics: Hydrostatic pressure and force on horizontal, vertical and inclined surfaces
Buoyancy and stability of floating bodies

units.title: Analysis of Pipe Flow
topics: Energy losses in pipelines; Darcy-Weisbach equation; minor losses
Hydraulic grade line and total energy line; concept of equivalent length
Pipes in series and parallel
Dimensionless numbers: Reynolds, Froude, Mach, Weber and Euler numbers

seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 CE 2-1 Fluid Mechanics: fluid properties & pressure, fluid statics, fluid kinematics, fluid dynamics (Bernoulli, momentum) and analysis of pipe flow.

seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 CE 2-2 Design Thinking & Innovation: elements & principles of design, design thinking process, innovation, product design and business processes.

subjects.notes: Course code 23ME57, 1-0-2-2 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Building Materials and Construction

units.title: Stones and Bricks
topics: Classification of rocks; characteristics of a good building stone; dressing of stones
Common building stones - properties, composition and uses
Bricks: composition, constituents of brick earth, manufacturing process
Characteristics of good bricks; classification; fly ash and hollow bricks

units.title: Lime and Cement
topics: Lime: limestone cycle, sources, properties, constituents, classification and storage
Cement: classification and properties; comparison between cement and lime
Constituents and functions of ingredients; outline of Portland cement manufacture
Field tests for cement; types, uses and storage of cements

units.title: Mortar and Masonry
topics: Classification and characteristics of good mortar; types and preparation; precautions
Types of masonry; joints in stone masonry
Bonds in bricks: English and Flemish bonds; tools for brick laying
Defects in brick masonry; importance of cavity and partition walls

units.title: Building Components
topics: Components of a building: substructure and superstructure
Importance, functions and requirements of good foundations; types of foundations
Lintels, arches and walls
Staircases; types of floors and roofs

units.title: Timber and Finishings in Buildings
topics: Classification of timber trees; cross section of an exogenous tree; seasoning of timber; types and uses; plywood
Paints: functions, types, constituents, characteristics and defects
Damp proofing: effects of dampness, methods
Materials used for damp-proof course and treatment in buildings

seo.title: Building Materials & Construction - CE 2-2
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 CE 2-2 Building Materials and Construction (audit): stones & bricks, lime & cement, mortar & masonry, building components, and timber & finishings.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.
